metadata.scope: Implemented live card pool including Bridge Set 0 and Anchor Identity Pass 1
notes: Authors are the visible identity layer.
Mechanic tags are the main synergy layer.
Support-family metadata remains optional and internal-facing.

mechanic_tags.name: tempo
mechanic_tags.definition: Accelerates pacing through card draw, inspiration gain, efficient hand refill, or other initiative-positive effects.

mechanic_tags.name: pressure
mechanic_tags.definition: Pushes damage races with aggressive statlines, direct reputation damage, or ways to bypass normal blocking.

mechanic_tags.name: control
mechanic_tags.definition: Removes, snipes, or sharply punishes opposing characters to swing board control.

mechanic_tags.name: reveal
mechanic_tags.definition: Looks at hidden cards or digs through deck information to improve selection and planning.

mechanic_tags.name: recursion
mechanic_tags.definition: Returns defeated or discarded cards to hand or otherwise recovers spent material.

mechanic_tags.name: buff
mechanic_tags.definition: Improves friendly character stats or memorability.

mechanic_tags.name: debuff
mechanic_tags.definition: Reduces enemy combat stats or weakens them for the turn.

mechanic_tags.name: defeat_trigger
mechanic_tags.definition: Generates value when the card is defeated or invites favorable sacrifice patterns.

mechanic_tags.name: knowledge
mechanic_tags.definition: Directly advances the knowledge track or converts literary progress into concrete gameplay value.

mechanic_tags.name: sustain
mechanic_tags.definition: Extends staying power through healing, sticky bodies, or other durability-oriented play patterns.

bridge_set_0.purpose: Deepen the weakest mechanic clusters without adding a new faction layer or large subsystem.
helper_logic_added: Per-turn revealedEnemyCards flag on each player

anchor_identity_pass_1.purpose: Bring the named signature cards up to the clarity level of the newer support shell without raising rules complexity.

revised_cards.name: Hamlet
revised_cards.archetype: Shakespeare Pressure Defeat, Knowledge Control Midrange

new_card_added.name: The Annotator
new_card_added.role: durable knowledge payoff (later replaced in the live pool)
new_card_added.reason: Knowledge needed a visible payoff card, but the later live-pool replacement moved that role onto a non-character knowledge plot for better flavor fit and simpler testing.

anchor_identity_pass_1.extra_sustain_reason: Stay of Execution already added a simple recovery tool, so this pass focused on clearer anchors first and leaves pre-defeat protection for future testing if it still proves necessary.

label_normalization.reason: Carroll-flavored support cards should reinforce the visible author layer instead of behaving like a parallel faction.
Useful internal grouping metadata, but not a player-facing identity.
Generic process-support cards, not authored identity cards.
Internal support metadata, not a visible faction layer.

cards.name: Hamlet
cards.author: Shakespeare
cards.notes: Now a Shakespeare reveal/knowledge engine that turns reflection into concrete setup.

cards.name: Macbeth
cards.author: Shakespeare
cards.notes: High-attack glass-cannon finisher.

cards.name: Margin Notes
cards.author: Neutral

cards.name: Read the Room
cards.author: Neutral

cards.name: Rough Draft
cards.author: Neutral

cards.name: Stay of Execution
cards.author: Neutral

cards.name: Dramatic Irony

cards.name: Close Reading
cards.author: Neutral
cards.notes: Threshold payoff plot that converts built-up knowledge into immediate card flow without needing a generic neutral character.

authors.name: Shakespeare
authors.notes: Now mixes tragic aggression with more deliberate information play thanks to Hamlet, Ophelia, and existing defeat-value pieces.

authors.name: Lewis Carroll
authors.notes: Fast draw, reveal payoffs, odd-angle pressure, and sticky value now feel much more like a real Carroll shell.

authors.name: Neutral
authors.notes: Carries the glue that lets cross-author shells form without becoming a faction of its own.

archetypes.name: Carroll Tempo Reveal
archetypes.notes: White Rabbit, Rabbit's Pocket Watch, Rough Draft, Read the Room, Alice, and Queen of Hearts now form a clearer fast-information shell.

archetypes.name: Shakespeare Pressure Defeat
archetypes.notes: Hamlet, Juliet, Lady Macbeth, Prospero, Ophelia, and Dramatic Irony make Shakespeare's pressure-value shell feel more intentional.

archetypes.name: Knowledge Control Midrange
archetypes.notes: Hamlet, Exposition, Soliloquy, Margin Notes, Close Reading, Critical Essay, and Stay of Execution now give the knowledge lane setup, payoff, and board presence.

archetypes.name: Sticky Recursion Value
archetypes.notes: Cheshire Cat, Yorick's Skull, Looking-Glass Return, and Dramatic Irony create a more reliable replay loop.

archetypes.name: Debuff Control
archetypes.notes: Crowd Murmur, Tea Party Chaos, A Pound of Flesh, and targeted removal now give debuff a real support package.

code_dependencies.details: Gameplay still only treats Shakespeare and Lewis Carroll as active authors.
Card UI shows the author field directly, so support labels still must stay out of author.
Bridge Set 0 adds a small per-turn revealedEnemyCards flag so reveal payoff cards can remain readable.
Literary-device quiz presentation still uses subtype and quiz metadata, not author.

remaining_gaps.details: Sustain is healthier now, but most protection still happens after damage rather than before a key unit would be defeated.
Knowledge now has a clearer threshold payoff, but testing still needs to confirm whether the package wants one more payoff or simply better tuning.
Macbeth, Jabberwock, and a few support-neutral cards are still simpler than the new anchor shell, though they are less urgent than the four cards addressed here.

ambiguities.issue: Future testing should confirm whether the 3+ Knowledge draw payoff is readable and worth the slot without becoming a generic auto-include.
Watch whether reveal-enabled direct writer damage feels exciting or too easy to spike when combined with cheap scouting.
Watch whether her immediate debuff is the right manipulation expression, or whether Shakespeare eventually wants a more ally-focused ambition payoff.

next_content_targets.need: If testing still shows fragile control shells collapsing too fast, add one simple pre-defeat protection card rather than a larger sustain package.
Tune whether the lane wants one more payoff card or just a numbers pass on Close Reading and Margin Notes.
Consider whether Macbeth or Jabberwock should be the next named anchors to receive a sharper identity pass.
Test whether the new neutral bridges are enough, then add at most one more cross-author connector instead of broadening into a bigger set.
